---
title: "ABS Fire"
author: "Solomon Maerowitz-McMahan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r process-and-display, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(stringr)
library(kableExtra)
library(officer)  # For Word output

CNP_clean<-read.csv('CNP_final.csv')

# Process the data
CNP_clean_processed <- CNP_clean %>%
  # Round numeric columns starting with 'C_' or 'N_' to 1 decimal point
 mutate(across(
  .cols = matches("^C_|^N_") & !starts_with("C_N_P_Ratio"),  # Exclude "C_N_P"
    ~ round(as.numeric(.), digits = 1)))%>%
  # Round all numeric columns to 1 decimal point and convert to scientific notation
  mutate( Percent_Phos_ = format(Percent_Phos_, scientific = TRUE),
          Percent_Phos_ = round(as.numeric(Percent_Phos_), digits = 4))%>%
          # Replace NA values with "--"
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "--", .))) %>%
  # Rename Percent_Phos_ to Phosphorus
  rename(P = Percent_Phos_,
         C= Carbon,
         N= Nitrogen,
         Frequency=Fire.Interval,
         Severity=Fire.Severity,
         C_N_P=C_N_P_ratio)%>%
  mutate(P = str_replace(P,'NA', "--") )%>%
  # Round all numeric columns to 2 digits and 1 decimal point
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., digits = 1)))%>%
  mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c(5, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 26, 29, 31, 34, 49, 56))) %>%
  arrange(Site) %>% 
  select(Sample, Frequency, Severity, C, N, P, C_N, C_P, N_P)

# # Add means and standard errors for numeric columns
# numeric_summary <- CNP_clean %>%
#   summarise(across(
#     where(is.numeric),
#     list(
#       mean = ~ mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
#       se = ~ sd(., na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(.)))
#     )
#   ))
# # Prepare summary rows for means and SE
# # Create summary rows for means and SE
# mean_row <- data.frame(
#   Site = "Mean",
#   Transect = "",
#   Fire.Interval = "",
#   Fire.Severity = "",
#   Carbon = round(numeric_summary$Carbon_mean, 2),
#   Nitrogen = round(numeric_summary$Nitrogen_mean, 2),
#   C_N = round(numeric_summary$C_N_mean, 2),
#   Phosphorus = round(numeric_summary$Percent_Phos__mean, 5),
#   C_P = round(numeric_summary$C_P_mean, 2)
# )
# 
# se_row <- data.frame(
#   Site = "SE",
#   Transect = "",
#   Interval = "",
#   Severity = "",
#   C = round(numeric_summary$Carbon_se, 2),
#   N = round(numeric_summary$Nitrogen_se, 2),
#   C_N = round(numeric_summary$C_N_se, 2),
#   P = round(numeric_summary$Percent_Phos__se, 5),
#   C_P = round(numeric_summary$C_P_se, 2)
# )
# 
# # Add the summary rows to the processed data
# CNP_clean_combined <- bind_rows(
#   CNP_clean_processed%>%mutate(P=as.double(P), C_P=as.double(C_P)),
#   mean_row,
#   se_row
# )
#view(CNP_clean_processed)
# Display the table with kable
CNP_clean_processed %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Table XXX Elemental composition of mycorrhizal fungal biomass collected",
    digits = 2,
    align = "c"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "20em") %>%  # Sample
  column_spec(2, width = "15em") %>%  # Frequency
  column_spec(3, width = "15em") %>%  # Severity
  column_spec(6, width = "15em") %>%  # P
  column_spec(9, width = "15em")      # C_N_P

# or I can just print as so

library(flextable)

# Convert table to a flextable object
ft <- CNP_clean_processed %>%
  flextable() %>%
  autofit()  # Adjust column width

# Save to Word
doc <- read_docx() %>%
  body_add_flextable(ft) %>%
  body_add_par("Table XXX Elemental composition of mycorrhizal fungal biomass collected", style = "heading 2", pos = 'before')

print(doc, target = "CNP_Table_Output.docx")
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
